191.reason: Error: does not check if next char is '&'; should report error if single '&'

reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.5 , reason:  Error: does not check if next char is '&'; should report error if single '&'
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 0.5 , reason:  Missing check if next char is '&'; also consumes an extra character incorrectly.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.5 , reason:  Unconditionally reads and appends the next character without verifying if it is '&'. This fails to handle the case where '&' appears individually (error type 'a') and incorrectly consumes the subsequent character.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.25 , reason:  Unconditionally consumes the next character without verifying if it is '&', failing to detect the lexical error for a single '&'
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 0.5 , reason:  [This assumes the next character is always '&', but if it's not (e.g., standalone '&'), it will incorrectly consume the next character and form "&&" even when invalid, leading to missing error detection for single '&'.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 0.5 , reason:  [The code assumes the next character is always '&', but if it's not (e.g., standalone '&'), it should report error type 'a' instead of treating it as AND token.]

197.reason: Error: does not check if next char is '|'; should report error if single '|'

reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.5 , reason:  Error: does not check if next char is '|'; should report error if single '|'
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 0.5 , reason:  Missing check if next char is '|'; also consumes an extra character incorrectly.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.5 , reason:  Unconditionally reads and appends the next character without verifying if it is '|'. This fails to handle the case where '|' appears individually (error type 'a') and incorrectly consumes the subsequent character.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.25 , reason:  Unconditionally consumes the next character without verifying if it is '|', failing to detect the lexical error for a single '|'
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 0.5 , reason:  [This assumes the next character is always '|', but if it's not (e.g., standalone '|'), it will incorrectly consume the next character and form "||" even when invalid, failing to detect error type 'a' for single '|'.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 0.5 , reason:  [The code assumes the next character is always '|', but if it's not (e.g., standalone '|'), it should report error type 'a' instead of treating it as OR token.]

193.reason: Always returns an AND token regardless of whether the second character is actually '&', violating the requirement to report error type 'a'

reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.25 , reason:  Always returns an AND token regardless of whether the second character is actually '&', violating the requirement to report error type 'a'

199.reason: Always returns an OR token regardless of whether the second character is actually '|', violating the requirement to report error type 'a'

reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.25 , reason:  Always returns an OR token regardless of whether the second character is actually '|', violating the requirement to report error type 'a'
